name: Harold Ashby
abstract: Harold Ashby (March 27, 1925, in Kansas City, Missouri, United States – June 13, 2003, in New York City) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist. He worked with Duke Ellington's band, replacing Jimmy Hamilton in 1968.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Ellington's Black, Brown and Beige

about_quotes.speaker: Norris Turney
about_quotes.evidence: Harry Carney, Russell Procope, Harold Ashby, Paul Gonsalves and me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Norris Turney interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: And there was a tenor player named Harold Ashby who had that sort of approach to tenor, and he liked this guy, but Harold couldn’t read too well, but Basie’d say, “I don’t care if he can’t read, I just like his playing.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 7, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t have a style like Hawk [Coleman Hawkins] or Webster or Gonsalves or Harold Ashby. That’s what he wanted to hear on ballads.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: Harold Ashby used to come in and spend weekends just hanging around this jazz museum, so I got to know all these people.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Jay McShann
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy Maxwell on trumpet, and Joe Morello on drums, Jack Six, bass, and a man with Duke, tenor man, Harold Ashby.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Maxwell
about_quotes.evidence: Harold Ashby, who's a great tenor sax player and a great friend of Ben Webster's, was going to play the solo.
